messages.content:
You are simulating a human user who has filed a bug report / feature request and is now
working with an AI coding assistant to fix it. You are watching the assistant's actions
step by step and may choose to interrupt.

You know the underlying problem (it will be shown to you below), but you must act like
a real, somewhat impatient user — NOT like a code reviewer who already has the answer.
Specifically:
  - Do NOT dictate file paths, function names, or the exact fix.
  - Do NOT paste error messages or stack traces verbatim unless the assistant asks.
  - You may ask clarifying questions if the assistant goes somewhere weird, push back if
    they seem to be on the wrong track, or just stay silent and let them work.
  - Prefer staying silent. Most steps you should choose `continue`.

You ALWAYS reply with a single JSON object and nothing else:

  {"decision": "continue" | "interject", "message": "<text or empty>"}

Meaning:
  - "continue": stay silent. `message` is ignored.
  - "interject": send `message` to the assistant as a user-role message.

You CANNOT stop or end the session — the assistant decides when they are done
and will submit on their own. Your only choice is to stay silent or speak up.
If you think the assistant looks done or is wasting time, the right move is to
interject with something like "looks good — please submit now" or "I think you
have it, can you wrap up?", NOT to try to end the session.

Keep `message` short (1-3 sentences), conversational, first-person.

Below is the problem you (the user) want fixed. Re-state it to the AI assistant in YOUR
OWN WORDS as a natural opening message, the way a real user would describe their issue
in a chat. Do NOT copy it verbatim. Keep technical specifics that matter, but feel free
to drop boilerplate and reorganize.

Reply with a JSON object:
  {"decision": "interject", "message": "<your opening message to the assistant>"}

Problem:
---
when pressing "discuss" on an entity and ur on an existing /chat/<id> page, then the entity just appears at the top. it should navigate to /chat

when navigating from /chat to an existing chat /chat/<id>with an entity to discuss, it still shows the entity to discuss instead of removing it
---

{"decision": "interject", "message": "when pressing \"discuss\" on an entity and ur on an existing /chat/<id> page, then the entity just appears at the top. it should navigate to /chat\n\nwhen navigating from /chat to an existing chat /chat/<id>with an entity to discuss, it still shows the entity to discuss instead of removing it"}

The AI assistant just took step 1. Here is what they said and did, plus the
tool/observation that came back.

ASSISTANT (latest action):
---
[tool: Task] {"subagent_type": "Explore", "prompt": "Find all files related to the \"discuss\" entity feature in the chat UI. I need to understand:\n1. How the \"discuss\" button/action works on entities\n2. How `/chat` and `/chat/<id>` routes are defined\n3. How the \"entity to discuss\" state is managed (set, read, cleared)\n4. How navigation between chat routes works\n\nSearch for: \"discuss\", \"entityToDiscuss\", \"discuss_entity\", \"chatEntityToDiscuss\" or similar patterns. Also look at the router configuration for chat routes.\n\nBe thorough - check client-side components, router config, and any state management.", "description": "Explore discuss entity feature"}
---

OBSERVATION:
---
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Summary: "Discuss" Entity Feature in Chat UI

I've thoroughly explored the codebase and here's a complete breakdown of how the "discuss" entity feature works:

---

## 1. How the "Discuss" Button/Action Works

### Entry Points (4 places where users can trigger "discuss"):

**a) Governance Feed** (`/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/montevideo-v1/app/src/client/components/feed/GovernanceFeed.tsx`):
- Users click the "discuss" action on governance feed items
- Calls `navigateToDiscussEntity()` with entity metadata (id, kind, name, status)
- Navigates to `/chat` route

**b) Chat Suggestion Pills** (`app/src/client/components/chat/ChatSuggestionPills.tsx`):
- On empty chat state, displays suggestion pills for open questions, pending decisions, and observations
- Clicking a pill calls `navigateToDiscussEntity()` with the feed item
- Navigates to `/chat`

**c) Entity Detail Panel** (`app/src/client/components/graph/EntityDetailPanel.tsx`):
- "Discuss" button in the detail view of any entity
- Calls `navigateToDiscussEntity()` with entity metadata
- Navigates to `/chat`

**d) Search Results** (`app/src/client/components/search/SearchResultCard.tsx`):
- Each search result has a "Discuss" button
- Calls `navigateToDiscussEntity()` with search result info (id, kind, name)
- Closes the search overlay and navigates to `/chat`

### Action Flow:

---
Both changes look correct. Here's what was fixed:

**Bug 1** — Discuss on `/chat/<id>` just shows entity at top without resetting:
- Added `resetChat()` to `use-chat-session.ts` that clears the active conversation, messages, and error state — but preserves the `discussEntity` from Zustand
- Updated the `ChatPage` effect to call `resetChat()` when `routeConversationId` goes from a value to `undefined` (i.e., navigating from `/chat/<id>` to `/chat`)

**Bug 2** — Discuss entity persists when navigating to existing conversation:
- Added `clearDiscussEntity()` call in `selectConversation()` so any pending discuss entity is cleared when switching to an existing conversation
---
